meta.title: Portolan — Publish geospatial data without the portal
meta.description: Portolan is an open-source toolkit and a set of conventions for publishing geospatial data as plain files on object storage. It builds on STAC, GeoParquet, and Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF, so governments and open data publishers can share datasets for roughly the cost of storage.

hero.title: Publish geospatial data
hero.description: Portolan is an open-source toolkit and a set of conventions for publishing geospatial data as plain files on object storage. It builds on STAC, GeoParquet, and Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF, so governments and open data publishers can share datasets for roughly the cost of storage.

open.title: Open
open.description: 100% open source and open formats, under open governance and an Apache-2.0 license. If Portolan disappeared tomorrow, your data would still work everywhere.

aiReady.title: AI-ready
aiReady.description: Agents read a Portolan catalog directly instead of scraping a portal, because the metadata is structured for retrieval. The <link>Finland.SDI demo</link> shows an agent answering questions against an SDI.

cheap.title: Cheap
cheap.description: Only two costs: storage and egress. A typical catalog runs on commodity object storage with nothing else to operate. The <link>cost calculator</link> estimates what that means for your data.

sovereign.title: Sovereign
sovereign.description: Your data lives in your bucket, on your cloud, in your jurisdiction. Host it on AWS, GCS, Azure, MinIO, Cloudflare R2, or any other S3-compatible storage.

scales.title: Scales to petabytes
scales.description: Clients fetch only the bytes they need through HTTP range requests, and object storage does the scaling. There is no Portolan server to size, tune, or keep alive.

breaks.title: Breaks the GIS silo
breaks.description: Every layer is a file at a stable URL. DuckDB, BigQuery, Pandas, and desktop GIS all read the same bytes, with no export step between teams.

howItWorks.title: Files on storage, not servers.
howItWorks.subtitle: Portolan replaces traditional SDI servers with cloud-native files on object storage. No database, no tile server, no portal software — browsers, query engines, and AI agents read the files directly.

convert.title: Convert
convert.description: Transform shapefiles, GeoTIFFs, and other formats into cloud-native equivalents: GeoParquet for vectors, COG for rasters, PMTiles for map tiles.

catalog.title: Catalog
catalog.description: Generate a Portolan catalog that describes every asset with structured metadata. It is built on STAC, so any STAC tool can read it.

publish.title: Publish
publish.description: Push the files to any S3-compatible object storage: AWS, GCS, Azure, R2, MinIO, or Source Cooperative for free open data hosting.

browse.title: Browse
browse.description: Open the result in the Portolan browser or any STAC viewer, query it with DuckDB, or point an AI agent at the catalog URL.

cli.name: portolan-cli
cli.title: The CLI
cli.description: Convert, validate, and sync. One command takes a folder of shapefiles to a browsable catalog on S3.
cli.compatibility: Works with AWS · GCS · Azure · R2 · MinIO · Source.coop

viewer.name: portolan-viewer
viewer.title: Browser-native viewer
viewer.description: A lightweight viewer that reads Portolan catalogs and cloud-native files directly, with no server behind it.

skills.name: portolan-skills
skills.title: AI agent skills
skills.description: Skills that let Claude, Gemini, and Codex agents publish catalogs and query cloud-native geospatial data.

quickstart.title: Quickstart
quickstart.intro: Browse an existing catalog, then publish your own data with the CLI or with an AI agent.

browse.title: Browse a catalog
browse.description: Portolan catalogs are live on the web. Open the browser to explore published datasets, or point DuckDB at any GeoParquet URL and query it in place.

cli.title: With the CLI
cli.description: Install portolan-cli, then init, add, check, and push. The CLI converts your files and generates the catalog.

claude.title: With Claude
claude.description: Install the Portolan skill in Claude Code and point it at your data. It handles conversion, metadata, and publishing.

registry.title: Browse {count} catalogs
registry.description: Discover and connect to Portolan catalogs from around the world.

cta.title: Have a catalog to share?
cta.description: Submit your Portolan catalog to the registry.

submit.title: Submit a catalog
submit.successDescription: Your catalog will appear after review.

map.infoBody: Basemap © CARTO, © OpenStreetMap contributors. Search powered by Nominatim. Catalogs appear as dots; zoom in to see their coverage areas.
map.noLocation: {count, plural, one {# catalog has no location data} other {# catalogs have no location data}}

resources.title: Early work, in the open.
resources.subtitle: Portolan is early-stage and developed in the open. These talks and demos show the approach and where it is heading; details will change.

holmesTalk.title: Portolan — Cloud-Native SDI
holmesTalk.description: Slides introducing the cloud-native SDI approach behind Portolan.

calculator.title: Cloud-Native SDI Cost Calculator
calculator.description: Estimate hosting costs for a catalog. Storage and egress are the only line items.

parksBuildings.title: Buildings in Dutch national parks
parksBuildings.description: Analysis demo joining buildings and park boundaries with PMTiles and DuckDB, straight from object storage.

monuments.title: Monuments in Dutch national parks
monuments.description: A second analysis demo over the same published data.

nextSdi.title: The Next SDI
nextSdi.attribution: Javier de la Torre · OGC Connect Helsinki
nextSdi.description: Keynote on where SDIs are heading: agents, cloud-native data, and fewer intermediaries.

finlandAgent.title: Finland.SDI agent demo
finlandAgent.description: A live webapp where an AI agent answers questions against a national SDI.

nlDemo.title: Portolan NL demo catalog
nlDemo.description: A working Portolan catalog of Dutch open data, served from object storage.

github.title: portolan-sdi on GitHub
github.description: The CLI, the registry, the specs, and this site, all in one org.
